extraction.one_line_summary: QUINTANA Nairo attacked 4km from summit to win stage, but SCHACHMANN defended yellow from BENOOT's late surge by 12 seconds.
tactical_patterns: EF placed BETTIOL in break but SCULLION chased in peloton, sacrificing break cooperation to control tempo for HIGUITA.
BENOOT attacked for GC despite teammate SAGAN chasing, revealing AG2R prioritized stage GC over sprint stage outcomes.
SCHACHMANN isolated early on final climb but GROSSSCHARTNER paced from behind, showing Bora tactical depth beyond visible.

rider_signals.SCULLION Tom: positive: pulled in peloton while teammate Bettiol sat in break, showing sophisticated dual-front team tactics
rider_signals.BENOOT Tiesj: positive: attacked for GC despite needing 36-second gap with bonifications, nearly succeeded (12s short)
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: positive: defended yellow while completely isolated on final climb, limiting Benoot to 47 seconds
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: neutral: refused to work in break with teammate chasing behind, pure tactical passenger role
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: decisive attack 4km from summit after crash setback stage 2, rebounded for stage win
rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: negative: chased own interests but aided Schachmann defense, tactical awareness failure cost Benoot potential yellow
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: present in front group but passive, missed podium in 4th despite Trek Segafredo positioning
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: started as virtual race leader 2min04s down, but dropped when Scullion attacked in break
